title: Best Indoor Grills of 2026: Smokeless That Delivers, Honest Sizing
excerpt: Which indoor grills actually deliver smokeless performance and realistic serving capacity for your home—from apartment balconies to family dinners.
author: Editorial Team
metaDescription: Which indoor grills actually deliver smokeless performance and realistic serving capacity for your home—from apartment balconies to family dinners.
intro:
Indoor grills promise the char and convenience of outdoor cooking right on your countertop, but two nagging realities persist: smoke that belies 'smokeless' claims and serving sizes that shrink when you actually place the food on the grate. Many models advertise four servings yet barely fit two burgers, while the term 'smokeless' often means 'less smoke than a campfire' rather than none at all.

The difference between a grill that becomes a countertop staple and one that collects dust often comes down to cleanability and heat control. A removable, dishwasher-safe plate and adjustable temperature transform the experience from a one-trick gadget into a nightly workhorse. Without these, you're left scrubbing awkward fixed grates and overcooking because there's no way to dial back the heat.

This guide matches you with the right balance of smokeless performance, real-world capacity, and multi-function versatility—so you can grill, sear, and even griddle with confidence, whether on a balcony or in a compact kitchen.

products.title: Ninja | Indoor Grill | Foodi | 5-in-1 (Grill, Air Fry, Roast, Bake, and Dehydrate | Fits up to 3lb Roast | Smoke Control System and Dishwasher Safe Parts| Black/Grey | AG301
products.label: Ninja Foodi 5-in-1 Indoor Grill with Air Fry, Roast, Bake & Dehydrate (AG301)
products.oneLiner: 5-in-1 powerhouse that sears at 500°F with cyclonic air, plus air fry and dehydrate.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Use the hood and splatter shield when grilling fatty meats, and run an overhead range fan to keep smoke from triggering alarms.

specs.label: Versatility
specs.value: Grill, air fry, bake, roast, dehydrate

pros: Replaces five appliances (grill, air fryer, roaster, oven, dehydrator) in one compact unit. — Five distinct cooking functions built into a single 15-inch footprint.
Ceramic nonstick grate is dishwasher-safe and releases stuck-on food with minimal effort. — Ceramic coating is PTFE/PFOA-free and designed for easy release.
Fast preheat to 500°F means less waiting; cyclonic air distributes heat evenly across the grate. — 1760W heating element and fan-driven air circulation for rapid, even heating.
Produces restaurant-quality sear marks and char on steaks, burgers, and vegetables. — 500°F maximum temperature with cyclonic air flow to replicate outdoor grilling.
One-touch functions and simple dial controls make switching between grill and air fry seamless. — No complex menus or smartphone pairing required.
cons: Higher upfront cost than many single-function grills, but the multi-function value offsets it over time.
At highest heat with fatty meats, smoke can be noticeable and may trigger smoke detectors without proper ventilation. — Smoke output is reduced by the hood and splatter shield but not eliminated.
products.verdict: For households ready to consolidate countertop clutter and willing to manage some smoke from fatty meats, this is the most capable all-in-one indoor grill available—it does what a dedicated grill does while adding air fry and dehydrate functions that justify the investment.
products.review:
The Ninja Foodi AG301 reaches 500°F using cyclonic air, delivering sear marks and char that rival an outdoor grill. Its five functions—grill, air fry, roast, bake, and dehydrate—cover nearly every cooking need, so you can crisp wings one night and dehydrate jerky the next without swapping machines.

Preheating is fast, usually under five minutes, and the ceramic nonstick surface releases food easily. The 10x10-inch grate fits four burgers or a large salmon fillet, and the grate is dishwasher-safe. When cooking fatty cuts at maximum heat, the unit can produce enough smoke to set off nearby detectors; using the included hood and splatter shield, plus an overhead fan, keeps things under control.

This appliance is aimed at households that want to replace multiple countertop gadgets—grill, air fryer, toaster oven, dehydrator—with a single 20-pound unit. It works best for cooks who are willing to ventilate the kitchen when searing high-fat proteins and who have counter space for its 15-inch depth. The premium price reflects the breadth of functions, not a budget-friendly entry point.

Build quality feels solid, with a ceramic-coated grate that resists scratching and a metal housing that doesn't wobble. The controls are straightforward dials and buttons—no app or digital guesswork. While the smoke-control system reduces output compared to open-contact grills, it is not a zero-smoke solution; the marketing language around “smoke control” is honest about reduction, not elimination.

For cooks who want one device that can grill a steak, air-fry frozen fries, and bake a small cake, the AG301 does it all without requiring separate appliance storage. It sacrifices nothing on cooking quality—the sear is deep and the air-fryer crisps evenly—while simplifying cleanup and countertop clutter.

💡 💡 Tip: Use the hood and splatter shield when grilling fatty meats, and run an overhead range fan to keep smoke from triggering alarms.

products.title: Cuisinart Griddler Indoor Grill, Panini Press, Sandwich Maker & More, 5-in-1 Grilling, Dishwasher-Safe Nonstick Cooking Plates, GR-4NNAS, Stainless Steel
products.label: Cuisinart Griddler GR-4NNAS
products.oneLiner: Dual-temperature 5-in-1 expandable to 240 sq in for griddling pancakes and paninis.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: For more even browning, flip food halfway through cooking or use the open-flat griddle mode for items that don't need top contact.
pros: Removable dishwasher-safe plates cut cleanup time to minutes.
Adaptable for grilling, griddling, and panini in one compact appliance.
Solid build and consistent cooking performance across a range of foods.
Intuitive dual temperature dials and simple operation, usable by anyone.
cons: Top plate runs cooler than bottom, leading to uneven browning on closed-cooked foods.
products.verdict: A practical everyday grill for families who value easy cleaning and multi-use cooking over perfect sear symmetry – the uneven top heat is a manageable tradeoff for the mid-range price.
products.review:
Unlike multi-function air fryer grills, the Cuisinart Griddler focuses on flat cooking with separate temperature controls for top and bottom plates. The 240 sq in surface opens flat for a griddle that handles pancakes, eggs, and patty melts, then closes for panini press action – all with a ceramic nonstick coating that releases food easily.

Cleaning is straightforward thanks to removable dishwasher-safe plates, and the dual temperature dials give independent control for each cooking zone. The tradeoff is that the top plate runs cooler than the bottom, which means foods may brown more on one side in closed mode – a quirk that matters most when you need even searing on thick cuts.

This Griddler suits households of 2–4 who want a single appliance for grilled cheese, pancakes, and thin steaks. The uneven heating is less noticeable for foods cooked open-flat or for paninis where you can flip the top piece. For consistent top-and-bottom browning on thick meats, a higher-heat model like the top pick is a better fit.

💡 💡 Tip: For more even browning, flip food halfway through cooking or use the open-flat griddle mode for items that don't need top contact.

products.title: George Foreman Smokeless - Digital Smart Select, Family Size (4-6 Servings), Stainless Steel, GRD6090B
products.label: George Foreman GRD6090B
products.oneLiner: Truly smokeless operation with digital time/temp controls and dishwasher-safe plate.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: For thicker cuts, preheat thoroughly and rely on the digital timer to avoid overcooking—gentler heat can still yield tender, evenly cooked results.
pros: Truly smokeless operation, minimal smoke and odor even with fatty foods — 48 mentions, 79% positive; many highlight no smoke even with steaks
Removable, dishwasher-safe grill plate makes cleanup effortless — 127 mentions, 94% positive
Simple, precise digital time and temperature controls — 69 mentions, 96% positive on ease of use
Even heating and fast cooking—burgers ready in about 5 minutes — 45 mentions, 80% positive on cooking speed
products.verdict: For families who prioritize smokeless convenience and digital precision, the GRD6090B delivers exactly what it promises. Those who demand steakhouse sear should look to higher-heat alternatives, but the tradeoff pays off every time the smoke alarm stays silent.
products.review:
The George Foreman GRD6090B stands out as one of the only indoor grills that genuinely eliminates smoke, not just reduces it. Where portable outdoor-ready electric grills lean on open-air dispersion, this sealed system traps and manages grease so thoroughly that even fatty burgers produce almost no visible smoke—no range hood needed. The digital interface brings a level of precision rare at this price, letting you set exact time and temperature without guesswork.

Cleanup is equally painless: the nonstick grill plate lifts off and goes straight into the dishwasher, and nearly every owner notes how little effort it takes to wipe clean. Preheat is fast, and burgers finish in about five minutes with even browning. The controls are intuitive, making weeknight chicken breasts or grilled vegetables a repeatable, low-fuss ritual.

This grill suits families who want quick, smokeless indoor meals without setting off alarms. Its 90 sq in surface holds about four standard burger patties at once—fine for 4–6 servings of thinner fare. The honest tradeoff is maximum temperature: it sits lower than some 500°F competitors, so thick steaks won’t develop the same crusty sear. If dinner often means ribeyes or thick-cut chops, a high-heat model will serve you better; for everyday burgers, chicken, and vegetables, the lower heat simply means less smoke and easier grease management.

💡 💡 Tip: For thicker cuts, preheat thoroughly and rely on the digital timer to avoid overcooking—gentler heat can still yield tender, evenly cooked results.

products.title: Ninja Griddle and Indoor Grill, 14’’, Electric Grill, For Steak, Burgers, Salmon, Veggies, and More, Pancake Griddle, Nonstick, Dishwasher Safe, 500F, Even Cooking, Silver, GR101
products.label: Ninja Sizzle GR101
products.oneLiner: 500°F edge-to-edge heating with interchangeable grill and griddle plates.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: For ribeye or sausages, turn on your range hood or crack a window; the low-smoke mesh lid helps but doesn’t eliminate all smoke.
pros: Edge-to-edge heat distribution eliminates cold spots for uniform cooking — 81% positive on heat distribution
Interchangeable grill and griddle plates handle everything from pancakes to porterhouse — 93% positive on functionality
Scratch-resistant nonstick surfaces release food easily and are dishwasher safe — 91% positive on cleaning
500°F maximum heat delivers a steakhouse sear that budget indoor grills cannot replicate — 94% positive on cooking
cons: High-fat foods can still produce noticeable smoke despite the mesh lid, making kitchen ventilation important — 41% negative on smoke level
products.verdict: If a dedicated high-heat grill and griddle for indoor searing is what you need—and you can live without air frying—the Ninja GR101 is a mid-range performer that earns its keep. Just plan to ventilate when cooking fatty cuts.
products.review:
The Ninja Sizzle GR101 focuses on one thing: blistering 500°F heat across a true 14-inch cooking surface. Unlike the all-in-one top pick that adds air frying and dehydrating, this model swaps multi-function for pure, even grilling and griddling. The interchangeable plates—one ridged grill, one flat-top griddle—let you move from seared ribeye to Sunday pancakes without a second appliance.

Cooking performance backs up the specs. The heating element delivers consistent edge-to-edge heat, bypassing the hot spots that leave one burger charred and another underdone. Both plates use a scratch-resistant nonstick coating that releases food cleanly and goes straight into the dishwasher. Cleanup is quick, and the compact footprint stows easily on the counter or in a cabinet.

Families of four to six and anyone who loves breakfast-for-dinner will get the most from this griddle-grill combo. High-fat foods can still produce noticeable smoke despite the perforated mesh lid—this is not a completely smokeless appliance. Running a range hood or opening a window is a small tradeoff for steakhouse-quality sear indoors.

💡 💡 Tip: For ribeye or sausages, turn on your range hood or crack a window; the low-smoke mesh lid helps but doesn’t eliminate all smoke.

products.title: Cuisinart Griddler® Deluxe, Electric Griddle, GR-150NAS
products.label: Runner-Up
products.oneLiner: Spacious 240-sq-in dual-zone with 500°F sear mode for crowd-feeding.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Use the sear mode in short bursts for grill marks, then switch to a high regular setting for longer browning—this works around the auto shutoff.
pros: Outstanding sear mode creates deep, consistent grill marks on steaks and sandwiches — 309 mentions, 82% positive on functionality
Large 240 sq in dual-zone surface cooks different foods at different temperatures side by side — 156 mentions, 100% positive on versatility
Ceramic nonstick plates clean easily in the dishwasher, saving serious scrubbing time — 405 mentions, 93% positive
Heats up quickly and maintains even temperature across the plate for reliable results — 151 mentions, 80% positive on heating speed
cons: Sear mode automatically ends after 2 minutes, requiring an extra press to continue if you need a longer sear — Mentioned in several top reviews; a usability friction point.
In contact-grill mode, the top plate may stay noticeably cooler than the bottom, leading to uneven browning on one side — 229 mentions, 35% negative on heat distribution.
products.verdict: If dual-zone flexibility and a spacious cooking surface matter more than flawless contact-grill symmetry, this Griddler turns out impressive batches with minimal cleanup—and the sear mode still delivers those eye-catching marks.
products.review:
Dual-zone temperature control and a dedicated 500°F sear mode turn this Griddler into a two-dish-at-once powerhouse. You can set the left plate to high for marinated chicken thighs while the right plate gently browns hash browns or keeps pancakes warm. The quick-heating 1800W element brings the surface to temperature fast, and the ceramic nonstick plates go straight into the dishwasher after the meal.

Unlike combo units that split focus between air frying and grilling, this model dedicates its entire design to griddle and grill functions. The 240-square-inch dual-zone layout offers more real surface than most dedicated indoor grills, letting you handle 8 burgers plus onions and peppers in one go. That focus means no air-fry basket to store or clean, just two large, reversible plates that lay flat for a massive griddle or lock into a contact grill.

The top plate runs cooler than the bottom in contact-grill mode, so food can brown more on the underside. Opening the unit flat eliminates the imbalance and turns it into a true double griddle—this is where it shines for big breakfasts and fajita spreads. The sear mode automatically cuts off after two minutes, which can break your rhythm when you want a sustained scorch on thick steaks, but it still delivers restaurant-quality grill marks on paninis, chops, and vegetables in short pulses.

💡 💡 Tip: Use the sear mode in short bursts for grill marks, then switch to a high regular setting for longer browning—this works around the auto shutoff.

products.title: George Foreman Indoor/Outdoor Electric Patio Grill, Apartment Approved, 15-Serving, Removable Stand, Black
products.label: George Foreman GIO2000BK
products.oneLiner: Apartment-approved electric grill with removable stand and 200 sq in for balcony BBQs.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Place the grill near a window or under a range hood to manage the light smoke from the open grate—no sealed smokeless system here.
pros: Quick to set up and intuitive to use, with indoor-outdoor flexibility for patios and camping. — 786 mentions, 98% positive
Apartment-friendly design needs no charcoal or propane, approved for balconies and covered decks. — 651 mentions, 98% positive

Heats up rapidly and spreads heat evenly, producing consistent grill marks across the 200 sq. in. grate. — 412 mentions, 89% positive on heating speed; 739 mentions, 92% positive on cooking performance
Well-built with a generous cooking area that handles family meals with ease. — 1388 mentions, 94% positive on quality; 466 mentions, 85% positive on size
cons: Only five manual heat settings, so fine-tuned temperature control isn't available for precision recipes.
Open-grate design can generate some smoke during indoor use, especially with higher-fat foods.
products.verdict: For balcony grillers and campers who want the feel of an open grate without charcoal, the George Foreman GIO2000BK delivers a capable, portable solution—just don't expect it to stay smokeless indoors.
products.review:
The George Foreman GIO2000BK stands apart from more compact, smokeless-oriented grills by embracing outdoor grilling. It ships with a detachable stand that turns the unit into a freestanding patio grill, making it legal for apartment balconies and covered decks where gas and charcoal are prohibited. Compared to the top pick's sealed smokeless system, this model uses an open grate that delivers more traditional char but demands ventilation and a watchful eye on smoke.

A 1500W heating element brings the grate to temperature quickly, and heat spreads evenly across the surface for consistent sear marks on burgers, chicken, and vegetables. The nonstick "George Tough" coating simplifies post-cook cleanup, though the grate itself isn't dishwasher safe, so hand-washing is needed.

With 200 sq. in. of cooking space, this grill accommodates family meals for 4–6. The same stand that enables outdoor use pops off for countertop grilling indoors, but the open-grate design means some smoke will escape, particularly with fatty meats. Temperature control is limited to five manual settings—adequate for general grilling, but anyone who wants digital precision for delicate proteins should look elsewhere.

💡 💡 Tip: Place the grill near a window or under a range hood to manage the light smoke from the open grate—no sealed smokeless system here.

products.title: Elite Gourmet EMG6505G 12" Smokeless Indoor Electric BBQ Grill with Glass Lid Dishwasher Safe, Nonstick, Adjustable Temperature, Fast Heat Up, Low-Fat Meals Easy to Clean Design
products.label: Elite Gourmet EMG6505G

products.verdict: If you’re a couple or solo diner grilling mostly vegetables, thin fish fillets, or chicken tenders and want a truly smokeless, easy-clean grill that won’t break the bank, this is a sensible pick.
products.review:
Elite Gourmet's 12-inch grill nails simplicity with its nonstick grate and dishwasher-safe parts that make post-meal cleanup fast. The 5-level temperature dial and glass lid give enough control for zucchini ribbons, asparagus, or thin chicken breasts, and the compact footprint disappears on small countertops.

The 1200W element keeps the price entry-level but trades off searing punch — thick steaks or bone-in cuts may need more time and won't develop deep char marks. For anyone whose indoor grilling centers on vegetables and lean, quick proteins, that's a minor trade-off; it keeps the appliance lightweight and apartment-friendly.

💡 💡 Tip: For thicker cuts, preheat for a full 5 minutes and rotate food regularly to compensate for slower heat recovery.

products.title: Hamilton Beach Electric Indoor Grill, 6-Serving, Large 90 sq. in. Nonstick Easy Clean Plates, Floating Hinge for Thicker Foods, 1200 Watts, 6.38"D x 12.68"W x 13.78"H , Silver (25371)
products.label: Hamilton Beach 25371
pros: Solid build quality holds up to occasional burger and hot dog sessions. — 188 mentions, 92% positive on quality
Heats up quickly and is simple to operate — just plug in and go. — 109 mentions, 95% positive on ease of use; 114 mentions, 86% positive on heating speed
Produces visible grill marks and cooks evenly enough for basic meals. — 133 mentions, 83% positive on cooking performance
cons: Fixed plates require manual scrubbing — no dishwasher shortcut for cleaning. — 262 mentions, 38% negative — cleaning challenges noted
No temperature dial means one fixed heat, so thin foods can over-brown before thicker items cook through. — 101 mentions, 71% negative — lack of temperature control noted
products.verdict: If you grill burgers and hot dogs a few times a month and don't mind spending a couple extra minutes wiping down fixed plates, this grill delivers basic results without a big spend.
products.review: The Hamilton Beach 25371 is a no-frills contact grill for occasional meals, heating up quickly to put decent sear marks on burgers and hot dogs. The build feels solid for a lightweight budget appliance, and the floating hinge handles thicker items without crushing them. Cleaning requires manual attention because the nonstick plates are fixed in place — you can't pop them in the dishwasher. Without adjustable temperature control, the plug-in operation runs at a fixed heat that can over-brown thinner items if you walk away. For regular users seeking the precise control and easy cleanup of a dial-controlled, dishwasher-safe grill, the slightly pricier smokeless models with removable plates offer a smoother daily experience.

products.title: Chefman Electric Smokeless Indoor Grill w/ Non-Stick Cooking Surface & Adjustable Temperature Knob from Warm to Sear for Customized BBQing, Dishwasher Safe Removable Water Tray, Black
products.label: Chefman Smokeless Grill
pros: Water tray design keeps cooking smokeless, even with fatty foods like bacon. — 212 mentions, 83% positive
Dishwasher-safe nonstick grate and drip tray make cleanup quick and easy. — 287 mentions, 90% positive
Simple dial operation lets you start grilling in seconds without complicated settings. — 181 mentions, 94% positive on ease of use
cons: Low 1120W wattage limits searing power—thick cuts can cook through without a char. — 141 mentions, 66% negative on heat output
products.verdict: For anyone cooking thin proteins and vegetables without any smoke, this budget-friendly grill is a capable apartment companion—just keep thick steaks for the grill outside.
products.review: The Cheffman Smokeless Grill uses a water tray under the grate to catch drippings, stopping smoke before it starts—bacon cooked indoors rarely triggers an alarm. The nonstick grate and drip tray go straight into the dishwasher, and the dial control makes it simple to start grilling quickly. The tradeoff is a modest 1120W heating element, which means thick steaks or bone-in chicken pieces can cook through without developing a deep char. This is a grill built for thin chicken breasts, fish, and vegetables rather than steakhouse marks, but it handles those lighter tasks without smoking up the kitchen.

buyingGuide.heading: Temperature Control: On/Off vs. Adjustable
buyingGuide.body:
On/off grills are simple and fast, but they deliver a single heat level that can scorch thin pieces while undercooking thick ones. Adjustable temperature dials or digital controls let you precisely hold a low simmer for vegetables or crank up to 500°F for a steakhouse sear.

Without the ability to modulate heat, you're forced to constantly lift the lid or guess doneness by time—a workable approach for quick paninis, but a recipe for disappointment with bone-in chicken or delicate fish.

buyingGuide.heading: Cooking Surface: Real-World Capacity
buyingGuide.body:
Ignore the '4-serving' or '6-serving' claims and look at the actual square inches. Many so-called 4-serving grills measure around 60–90 sq in, which fits only two average-sized burgers. For a family of four, aim for at least 100 sq in.

Open flat designs like the Cuisinart Griddler double the usable area by transforming into a griddle, giving you 240 sq in of versatile cooking space. Contact grills with fixed hinges lose half that flexibility.

buyingGuide.heading: Smokeless Operation: Water Trays & Airflow
buyingGuide.body:
True smokeless grills rely on a water tray or powerful cool-air zone to catch drips before they burn. Fat hitting a hot element vaporizes into smoke; a water tray submerges the drippings, while advanced airflow systems cool the smoke chamber.

Even the best systems can't eliminate every wisp when searing bacon-wrapped steaks—keep a window open or run the range hood, and remember that 'smokeless' means 'dramatically reduced,' not 'zero smoke.'

buyingGuide.heading: Removable Plates: The Cleanup Reality
buyingGuide.body:
Removable, dishwasher-safe plates save you from crouching over the sink with a damp sponge, trying to scrub grease out of electrical crevices. Fixed plates accumulate carbonized residue over time, leading to off-flavors and discouraging daily use.

If you grill frequently, opt for ceramic nonstick coatings that are PTFE/PFOA-free and release food easily. A model without removable plates becomes a chore that eventually ends up in the back of the cabinet.

buyingGuide.heading: Wattage & Maximum Heat: Searing Power
buyingGuide.body:
Wattage directly dictates heat recovery speed and ultimate temperature ceiling. A 1200W grill may take twice as long to reheat after you place cold food on the grate, resulting in steaming instead of searing. For thick steaks and pork chops, target 1500W or higher.

Maximum temperature matters just as much; 450°F can produce grill marks, but 500°F creates the deep, restaurant-quality char that truly mimics outdoor cooking.

buyingGuide.heading: Versatility: Dedicated Grill vs. Multi-Function
buyingGuide.body:
A dedicated grill excels at one thing—high heat and char—without occupying extra counter space. Multi-function models add air frying, baking, or dehydrating, effectively replacing several appliances. This is a win for space-saving kitchens, but the added complexity can compromise the pure grilling experience.

Consider your cooking habits: if you make pancakes for Saturday breakfast and then grill burgers for dinner, a model with interchangeable griddle plates or multiple cooking modes will earn its keep. If you only need a weeknight steak searer, stick with a purpose-built grill.

faq.question: Why does my smokeless electric grill still produce smoke?
faq.answer: No electric grill is 100% smoke-free because fat drips inevitably hit heated surfaces. True smokeless models use a water tray or cool-air chamber to capture drips before they burn, but high-fat foods like bacon or marbled steak can still produce wisps. Run a range hood or crack a window to carry away trace smoke.

faq.question: How many burgers can a 4-serving indoor grill actually fit?
faq.answer: Most '4-serving' grills squeeze in only two average-sized burgers, because the stated capacity refers to total cooking area, not the usable flat surface. Look for at least 100 square inches for a family of four, or opt for an open-flat design that doubles the space.

faq.question: Do I need a grill with temperature control or is an on/off model enough?
faq.answer: On/off models work for simple sandwiches and thin burgers, but they tend to overheat delicate foods and can't maintain a gentle simmer. Adjustable temperature—whether a dial or digital control—is essential for thicker meats, varied recipes, and preventing scorching.

faq.question: How do I clean an indoor grill without removable plates?
faq.answer: Without removable plates, unplug the grill, let it cool, then carefully wipe the grates with a damp cloth or sponge, avoiding the electrical housing. Some fixed-plate models have a grease drain spout. If daily scrubbing sounds tedious, invest in a model with dishwasher-safe plates.
